_meta.description: Complete parameter correlation matrix — all known relationships between OBD-II parameters based on automotive engineering principles, engine control system logic, physics of ICE, and manufacturer calibration strategies

field_notes.diagnosticWeight: 1-10, how important this correlation is for diagnosis (10 = highest)
field_notes.correlationType: positive | negative | proportional | inverse | conditional | complex

correlations.name: Load vs Throttle Position
correlations.description: Engine load should increase with throttle opening. This is the fundamental driver demand correlation.
correlations.formula: Load ≈ f(TPS, RPM, VE)
correlations.whenHealthy: Load increases smoothly with throttle opening
correlations.whenAbnormal: Throttle opens but load stays low, or load high despite low throttle
possibleCauses: Vacuum leak causing high load at closed throttle
Clogged air filter limiting load increase

correlations.name: MAF vs Calculated Load
correlations.description: MAF reading should match calculated engine load. Validates MAF sensor accuracy.
correlations.formula: MAF(g/s) ≈ (RPM × Displacement × VE × Air Density) / 3456
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF reading matches expected airflow for current load
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF reading deviates significantly from expected value
possibleCauses: Air leak between MAF and throttle body
MAF sensor element contaminated with oil

correlations.name: RPM vs Vehicle Speed
correlations.description: RPM/Speed ratio indicates gear selection and transmission function.
correlations.formula: Speed = (RPM × Tire Circumference) / (Final Drive × Gear Ratio × 60)
correlations.whenHealthy: Consistent RPM/Speed ratio within each gear
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM high for speed (slipping) or speed erratic (VSS issue)
possibleCauses: Incorrect tire size affecting speed reading

correlations.name: Combined Fuel Trim Health
correlations.description: STFT + LTFT indicates total fuel system correction. Both together reveal severity of fuel delivery deviations.
correlations.formula: Total Correction = STFT + LTFT (should be near 0%)
correlations.whenHealthy: Combined trims stay within ±10%
correlations.whenAbnormal: Combined trims exceed ±15%, indicating fuel system issue
possibleCauses: Positive trims: Vacuum leak, low fuel pressure, clogged injector, air leak after MAF
Negative trims: Leaking injector, high fuel pressure, saturated EVAP canister
Oscillating: O2 sensor response issue, injector balance problem

correlations.name: Bank 1 vs Bank 2 Fuel Trim
correlations.description: Bank trim comparison identifies bank-specific issues on V-engines.
correlations.formula: |LTFT_B1 - LTFT_B2| should be < 5%
correlations.whenHealthy: Both banks require similar fuel correction
correlations.whenAbnormal: One bank running significantly leaner/richer than other
possibleCauses: Intake manifold gasket leak on one bank
Exhaust leak on one bank before O2 sensor
Head gasket seepage on one bank
O2 sensor reading difference between banks

correlations.name: O2 Sensor vs Fuel Trim Response
correlations.description: O2 voltage changes should cause opposite fuel trim changes.
correlations.formula: High O2 (>0.7V) → Negative STFT, Low O2 (<0.3V) → Positive STFT
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel trims respond correctly to O2 sensor readings
correlations.whenAbnormal: No fuel trim response to O2 changes, or wrong direction

correlations.name: Pre-Cat vs Post-Cat O2 Activity
correlations.description: Post-cat O2 should be much steadier than pre-cat O2. Rapid switching post-cat = degraded catalyst.
correlations.formula: Post-cat O2 switching rate should be < 10% of pre-cat rate
correlations.whenHealthy: Pre-cat O2 switches rapidly, post-cat O2 stays near 0.6V
correlations.whenAbnormal: Post-cat O2 mirrors pre-cat O2 activity

correlations.name: Timing Advance vs Engine Load
correlations.description: Timing should retard as load increases to prevent knock.
correlations.formula: Timing ≈ Base Timing + f(RPM) - f(Load) - Knock Retard
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing decreases smoothly as load increases
correlations.whenAbnormal: Excessive timing at high load (knock risk) or no timing at low load (power loss)
possibleCauses: MAF sensor causing incorrect load calculation

correlations.name: Timing Advance vs RPM
correlations.description: Timing should advance with RPM to optimize burn rate.
correlations.formula: Timing advance increases roughly 1° per 500 RPM
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing advances smoothly with RPM increase
correlations.whenAbnormal: Flat timing curve or erratic timing changes

correlations.name: Misfire Activity vs Fuel Trim
correlations.description: Misfires often correlate with fuel trim extremes. Lean misfire = high positive trim. Rich misfire = high negative trim.
correlations.formula: Lean misfire: High positive trim + misfire. Rich misfire: High negative trim + misfire
correlations.whenHealthy: No misfires with normal fuel trims
correlations.whenAbnormal: Misfires increase with fuel trim extremes
possibleCauses: Lean misfire: Vacuum leak, clogged injector, low fuel pressure
Rich misfire: Fouled plug, ignition weak, flooding
Random misfire: Ignition component failure, compression issue

correlations.name: Coolant vs Intake Air Temp Warm-up
correlations.description: Both temperatures should rise together during warm-up. ECT rises faster due to engine heat generation.
correlations.formula: ECT should rise faster than IAT, both starting near ambient
correlations.whenHealthy: ECT rises steadily, IAT follows with heat soak
correlations.whenAbnormal: ECT not rising (thermostat stuck open) or IAT stuck
possibleCauses: Head gasket leak affecting temp readings

correlations.name: Coolant vs Oil Temperature
correlations.description: Oil temp should track slightly behind coolant temp, running 10-20°C higher under load.
correlations.formula: Oil temp ≈ Coolant temp + 10-20°C under load
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil temp follows coolant, slightly higher under load
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil temp significantly higher than coolant

correlations.name: Coolant Temp vs Fuel Trim During Warmup
correlations.description: Fuel trims should normalize as engine warms up. Cold = richer mixture. Warm = normalized trims.
correlations.formula: Cold: Richer mixture (negative trim adjustment). Hot: Normalized trims
correlations.whenHealthy: Trims stabilize as engine reaches operating temp
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trims don't stabilize or are extreme when warm

correlations.name: Catalyst Temp vs Engine Load
correlations.description: Catalyst temperature increases with sustained load. Above 900°C indicates misfire or rich condition.
correlations.formula: Cat temp rises 50-100°C above exhaust temp under load
correlations.whenHealthy: Cat temp rises with load but stays below 850°C
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cat temp excessive (>900°C) indicating misfire or rich condition

correlations.name: MAP vs Throttle Position
correlations.description: Manifold pressure should increase (toward atmospheric) with throttle opening.
correlations.formula: MAP approaches 101 kPa (atmospheric) as throttle opens
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP increases smoothly from ~30-40 kPa at idle toward 101 kPa at WOT
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP stays high at closed throttle or doesn't reach atmospheric at WOT

correlations.name: MAF vs RPM at Steady State
correlations.description: MAF should increase roughly proportionally with RPM at constant load.
correlations.formula: MAF doubles when RPM doubles at same load
correlations.whenHealthy: Linear relationship between MAF and RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF doesn't scale with RPM properly

correlations.name: Boost Pressure vs Throttle (Turbo)
correlations.description: Boost should build with throttle opening on turbo vehicles. No boost = turbo/wastegate failure.
correlations.formula: Boost builds after ~2500 RPM, reaches target at WOT
correlations.whenHealthy: Smooth boost build matching throttle input
correlations.whenAbnormal: No boost, slow boost build, or overboost

correlations.name: Turbo RPM vs Engine RPM
correlations.description: Turbo shaft speed correlates with engine RPM and load.
correlations.formula: Turbo RPM = f(Engine RPM, Load, Wastegate Position)
correlations.whenHealthy: Turbo spools up proportionally with engine load
correlations.whenAbnormal: Turbo not spooling or overspeeding

correlations.name: EGR Command vs MAP Response
correlations.description: Opening EGR should increase MAP (reduce vacuum). No MAP change = EGR stuck closed.
correlations.formula: MAP increases 5-15 kPa when EGR opens 20-40%
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP rises when EGR commanded, returns when closed
correlations.whenAbnormal: No MAP change with EGR command

correlations.name: EGR Activity at Idle
correlations.description: EGR should be closed at idle for stability. Active EGR at idle causes rough running.
correlations.formula: EGR should be 0% at idle
correlations.whenHealthy: EGR stays closed at idle, RPM stable
correlations.whenAbnormal: EGR open at idle causing rough running

correlations.name: EVAP Purge vs Fuel Trim Response
correlations.description: Fuel trims should shift slightly rich when purging hydrocarbons from canister.
correlations.formula: STFT goes slightly negative (1-5%) when purge active
correlations.whenHealthy: Small negative fuel trim shift during purge
correlations.whenAbnormal: No fuel trim change or large rich shift during purge
possibleCauses: No change: Purge valve stuck closed, empty canister
Large shift: Saturated canister, liquid fuel in EVAP

correlations.name: Trans Fluid Temp vs Engine Load
correlations.description: Transmission temperature increases with sustained load.
correlations.formula: Trans temp rises 1-2°C per minute under sustained high load
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp stabilizes around 80-100°C normal driving
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp climbing rapidly or exceeding 120°C

correlations.name: Gear Selection vs RPM/Speed Ratio
correlations.description: Each gear has a specific RPM/Speed ratio. Deviation indicates slipping or wrong gear.
correlations.formula: Expected RPM = Speed × Gear Ratio × Final Drive / Tire Rev
correlations.whenHealthy: RPM matches expected value for gear and speed
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM higher than expected (slipping) or wrong gear

correlations.name: TPS1 vs TPS2 Cross-Check
correlations.description: Dual TPS sensors should track each other. Disagreement is a safety issue on drive-by-wire vehicles.
correlations.formula: TPS1 ≈ TPS2 within 2%
correlations.whenHealthy: Both TPS sensors reading identically
correlations.whenAbnormal: TPS sensors disagree — potential safety issue

correlations.name: MAP vs Barometric Pressure
correlations.description: MAP should never exceed barometric pressure on naturally aspirated engines.
correlations.formula: MAP ≤ Barometric Pressure (NA engines)
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP always below or equal to barometric
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP exceeds barometric (sensor error or boost on NA)

correlations.name: O2 Sensor Activity vs Time
correlations.description: O2 sensors should start switching within 30 seconds of start. Delayed activity = heater failure.
correlations.formula: O2 should be switching before ECT reaches 60°C
correlations.whenHealthy: O2 sensor active and switching early in warm-up
correlations.whenAbnormal: O2 sensor slow to respond or not switching

correlations.name: DPF Differential Pressure vs Engine Load
correlations.description: DPF backpressure increases with exhaust flow (load). High DP = soot accumulation needing regen.
correlations.formula: DPF DP increases with exhaust volume
correlations.whenHealthy: Proportional increase with load, within limits
correlations.whenAbnormal: Excessive pressure drop indicating high soot load

correlations.name: DPF Temp During Regeneration
correlations.description: DPF temperature should rise significantly during active regen (550-650°C).
correlations.formula: DPF temp reaches 550-650°C during active regen
correlations.whenHealthy: Controlled temperature rise during regen
correlations.whenAbnormal: Temp not reaching regen threshold or runaway

correlations.name: System Voltage vs Electrical Load
correlations.description: Voltage should remain stable (13.5-14.8V) despite electrical load changes.
correlations.formula: Voltage should stay 13.5-14.8V regardless of load
correlations.whenHealthy: Stable voltage across all conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage dropping under load or not recovering

correlations.name: Oil Pressure vs Engine RPM
correlations.description: Oil pressure should increase with RPM due to pump output. Low pressure at high RPM = pump wear or bearing failure.
correlations.formula: Oil pressure roughly doubles from idle to 3000 RPM
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil pressure rises smoothly with RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil pressure not increasing with RPM

correlations.name: Oil Pressure vs Oil Temperature
correlations.description: Oil pressure decreases as oil warms due to viscosity change. Excessive drop when hot = bearing wear.
correlations.formula: Oil pressure drops 20-40 kPa as oil goes from cold to hot
correlations.whenHealthy: Moderate pressure drop as oil warms
correlations.whenAbnormal: Excessive pressure drop when hot

correlations.name: Volumetric Efficiency Validation
correlations.description: Cross-check MAF, RPM, and displacement for VE calculation. Allows predicting ideal MAF vs actual.
correlations.formula: VE = (MAF × 3456) / (RPM × Displacement × Air Density)
correlations.whenHealthy: Calculated VE is 85-100% at WOT
correlations.whenAbnormal: VE significantly different from expected

correlations.name: Injector Duty Cycle vs Load
correlations.description: Injector duty cycle should match engine load demand. IDC maxing out = fuel system limitation.
correlations.formula: IDC = (Pulse Width × RPM) / 1200. Should be <85% at WOT.
correlations.whenHealthy: IDC below 85% at WOT (headroom for enrichment)
correlations.whenAbnormal: IDC maxing out, indicates fuel system limitation
